comments.text: Thank you for the clarification and for pointing out the original conditions in the Erdős–Hajnal list. Even with the condition \(\gamma \ge 2\), however, a size obstruction can still occur: if some \(\kappa_\alpha > \lambda\), then the conclusion \(\lambda \to (\kappa_\alpha)^r_{\alpha<\gamma}\) would require a subset of \(\lambda\) of size \(\kappa_\alpha > \lambda\), which is impossible, while the hypothesis might still hold non-vacuously. Thus, to ensure the problem is nontrivial in ZFC, it seems necessary to also require \(\kappa_\alpha \le \lambda\) for all \(\alpha < \gamma\) (or at least for those \(\alpha\) that could correspond to the color of a large homogeneous set in the stepping-up coloring). In any case, the literal unrestricted statement — without any bound on \(\kappa_\alpha\) relative to \(\lambda\) — remains false, as my minimal counterexample shows. The intended formulation (with \(\gamma \ge 2\), \(r < \omega\), \(\kappa_\alpha > r\), and presumably \(\kappa_\alpha \le \lambda\)) is the interesting open problem. I appreciate your maintaining this valuable list of problems and your careful attention to these details.

I uploaded a short note pointing out that the \emph{literal} fully-quantified statement of Erdős--Hajnal--Rado Problem 1167 (as written in the book/problem list) is falsified by a simple size obstruction. If one allows some target $\kappa_\alpha>\lambda$, then the conclusion\[ \lambda\to(\kappa_\alpha)^r_{\alpha<\gamma} \]is impossible, while the hypothesis can still hold. In fact, taking $\gamma=1$ and $\kappa_0=\lambda^+$, the premise\[ 2^\lambda\to(\kappa_0+1)^{r+1}_{<1} \]is automatic (a coloring into $<1$ colors is vacuous; equivalently it just requires $2^\lambda\ge \kappa_0+1$), but the conclusion would require a subset of $\lambda$ of size $\lambda^+$, contradiction. This suggests the intended nontrivial formulation should explicitly assume $\kappa_\alpha\le\lambda$ for all $\alpha<\gamma$; under this restriction the general heterogeneous implication still seems open (beyond the classical uniform stepping-up/stepping-down consequences for $r\ge3$). Any references, known partial results, or corrections to the intended statement of \#1167 would be very welcome. DOI: 10.13140/RG.2.2.22305.06242

This is also Problem 2 in the Erdős-Hajnal problem list, which gives the additional conditions that $\gamma \geq 2$ (it actually says $\gamma \leq 2$, but I assume this is an obvious typo). It also has the additional conditions $r<\omega$ and $\kappa_\alpha >r$. Some known special cases are listed there (I'll update this on the site soon). The condition $\kappa_\alpha\leq \lambda$ does not appear there, however - presumably $\gamma\geq 2$ suffices to rule out the vacuous problem you noted.
